nvt.name: Apache Log4j Remote Code Execution (Log4Shell)
nvt.solution: Update Apache Log4j to version 2.17.0 or later.

result.description: The remote host is running Apache Log4j version 2.x which is vulnerable to remote code execution via JNDI lookup. An attacker can exploit this by sending a specially crafted request containing a JNDI reference that triggers arbitrary code execution on the server.

nvt.name: SQL Injection in Login Form
nvt.solution: Use parameterized queries and input validation.

result.description: The login form at /api/login is vulnerable to SQL injection. The username parameter is directly concatenated into SQL queries without sanitization, allowing an attacker to bypass authentication or extract database contents.

nvt.name: SSL/TLS: OpenSSL CCS Injection Vulnerability
nvt.solution: Update OpenSSL to version 1.0.1h or later.

result.description: The remote host is affected by a vulnerability in OpenSSL that allows a man-in-the-middle attacker to use a CCS injection attack to decrypt and modify traffic.

nvt.name: OpenSSH Multiple Vulnerabilities
nvt.solution: Update OpenSSH to version 9.4 or later.

result.description: The version of OpenSSH installed on the remote host is prior to 9.4. It is therefore affected by multiple vulnerabilities including PKCS#11 provider code execution.

nvt.name: WordPress Core XSS Vulnerability
nvt.solution: Update WordPress to the latest version.

result.description: WordPress version detected on the remote host contains a stored cross-site scripting vulnerability in the comment functionality that could allow attackers to execute arbitrary JavaScript.

nvt.name: SSL/TLS: Server Supports Weak Cipher Suites
nvt.solution: Disable weak cipher suites in the SSL/TLS configuration.

result.description: The remote SSL/TLS server supports cipher suites that are considered weak. These include RC4-based and DES-based ciphers that provide insufficient security.

nvt.name: Missing HTTP Security Headers
nvt.solution: Add X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Content-Security-Policy, and Strict-Transport-Security headers.

result.description: The remote web server does not send several important HTTP security headers including X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, and Content-Security-Policy, leaving users vulnerable to clickjacking and MIME-type confusion attacks.

nvt.name: SNMP Agent Default Community Name (public)
nvt.solution: Change the default SNMP community string and restrict SNMP access to authorized management stations.

result.description: The remote SNMP agent is using the default community string 'public' which allows anyone to read device configuration and network information.

nvt.name: SSH Weak Key Exchange Algorithms
nvt.solution: Disable weak key exchange algorithms such as diffie-hellman-group1-sha1.

result.description: The SSH server supports weak key exchange algorithms that could potentially be exploited in targeted attacks. While not immediately critical, these should be disabled as part of security hardening.

nvt.name: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure
nvt.solution: Filter ICMP timestamp requests at the firewall level.

result.description: The remote host responds to ICMP timestamp requests, which could allow an attacker to determine the system time and potentially use this information in time-based attacks.
